title: Pip and Glimmer and the Night Watch

pageTimings.text:
One morning, things had moved in the night.
Pip's boots were not where he had left them.
A bench was tipped over.
Someone had been in the clearing.

The next night, it happened again.
More things had moved.
"Was it you?" asked Stone.
"No," said Pip.
"Was it you?" asked Pip.
"No," said everyone.

"I am going to find out," said Pip.
He told Glimmer his plan.
They would stay awake.
They would watch the clearing.
Glimmer said yes immediately.

Night fell.
Moonwood glowed softly.
Pip and Glimmer hid behind the big mushroom.
They watched.
Nothing happened.

It was very late.
The clearing was empty.
Glimmer's eyes kept closing.
"Don't fall asleep," whispered Pip.
"I am not asleep," said Glimmer.
Glimmer was almost asleep.

Then — a sound.
A shuffling.
A round shape moving through the clearing.
Pip and Glimmer looked at each other.
They crept closer.

It was Burrow.
His eyes were closed.
He was fast asleep.
He was walking.

Burrow bumped the bench.
It fell over.
Burrow kept walking.
He found Pip's door.
He moved the boots with his paws.
He did not wake up.

He found the acorn biscuits.
He put some in his pocket.
He was still asleep.
Glimmer covered their mouth.

Then Burrow sat down.
Against the big mushroom.
Pip and Glimmer were still behind it.
Burrow began to snore.

In the morning, Burrow woke up.
He looked around.
Pip and Glimmer were standing in front of him.
"Good morning," said Burrow.
There was a long pause.

"It was you," said Pip gently. "You sleep-dig."
"I am very sorry," said Burrow.
He put his boot by the door.
"Will that stop you?" asked Glimmer.
"I don't know," said Burrow.
"I am asleep."
